question_title: Will OpenAI file an initial registration statement (S-1) with the SEC to launch an IPO, before March 15, 2026?
summary: OpenAI has not filed an S-1 with the SEC as of February 7, 2026, with only ~36 days remaining before the March 15 deadline. All reporting indicates OpenAI's CFO targets a 2027 listing with H2 2026 as the earliest possible filing timeline. The company is still in informal bank discussions and hasn't completed prerequisite steps like a major fundraising round. Manifold prices an even broader "IPO by March 2026" question at just 1.4%. The Metaculus CP is 10%, which seems generous given the overwhelming evidence against an imminent filing. This is essentially a question of whether an extremely unlikely surprise occurs in the next 5 weeks.

factors.description: No S-1 filed on EDGAR as of today - with only 36 days remaining, the default trajectory is NO
OpenAI CFO Sarah Friar targeting 2027 listing; H2 2026 is earliest consideration for filing with regulators - well past the March 15 deadline
Company still in 'informal discussions' with Wall Street banks - not in active filing mode for near-term S-1
Manifold market for 'OpenAI IPO by end March 2026' at 1.4% probability
Major prerequisites incomplete: need to finalize fundraising round, select underwriters, prepare financials for public disclosure
Metaculus community prediction at 10% - slightly higher than evidence warrants but reflects some residual uncertainty

resolution_reason: The question resolves NO. I searched the SEC EDGAR database for S-1 filings containing "openai" from January 2025 through March 15, 2026, and found no S-1 registration statement filed by OpenAI or any subsidiary involved in ChatGPT. The 47 results found were S-1 filings from other companies (CoreWeave, Arrive AI, etc.) that merely mention OpenAI in their documents. News reports consistently indicate OpenAI is targeting a filing "as soon as the second half of 2026" with a 2027 IPO, which is after the March 15, 2026 deadline. The deadline has now passed with no OpenAI S-1 filing on record.
resolution_criteria: This question resolves as **Yes** if OpenAI, or any subsidiary directly involved in the creation or commercialization of ChatGPT, files a public, non-confidential [SEC Form S-1](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/s/sec-form-s-1.asp) at the U.S. Security and Exchange Commission's [EDGAR](https://www.sec.gov/search-filings) database with a filing date before March 15, 2026.
fine_print:
Please note that this question asks about the filing of an S-1. Whether the IPO actually occurs does not affect resolution.

***
This question's information (resolution criteria, fine print, background info, etc) is synced with an [original identical question](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/41205) which opened on 2025-12-16 13:00:00. This question will resolve based on the resolution criteria and fine print contained above. However, if this question would resolve differently than the original question, then this question will be annulled. Additionally, if the original question's resolution could have been known before this question opened, then this question will be annulled.
background_info:
From [Invezz](https://in.investing.com/news/stock-market-news/spacex-to-openai-mega-ipos-set-to-debut-in-2026-5152303) via Investing.com: 

> OpenAI is laying early groundwork for an IPO that could value the company at up to \$1 trillion.

> The ChatGPT maker is considering filing with regulators as early as the second half of 2026, with CFO Sarah Friar indicating to associates that the company is targeting a 2027 listing, though advisers say it could come sooner.

> The potential offering follows a significant restructuring completed this week, creating a new corporate structure under the OpenAI Foundation, which now holds a 26% stake.

> OpenAI’s rapid revenue growth—expected to reach an annualized run rate of \$20 billion by year-end—is matched by mounting losses and rising capital needs, particularly as CEO Sam Altman pursues multi-trillion-dollar investments in AI infrastructure.
